calendar.description: House Business, Labor, and Commerce Hearing

general_provisions: This bill modifies the scope of practice requirements for health professions.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>defines terms;<hr><ltbullet>provides that a physical therapist may prescribe durable medical equipment;<hr><ltbullet>expands the medical imaging a physical therapist may order;<hr><ltbullet>allows a physical therapist assistant to perform limited joint mobilization;<hr><ltbullet>provides a certified registered nurse anesthetist prescriptive authority immediately before and after a procedure;<hr><ltbullet>extends the amount of time a student may work as a registered nurse apprentice;<hr><ltbullet>requires a minimum level of registered nursing experience before licensure as an advanced practice registered nurse;<hr><ltbullet>permits an advanced practice registered nurse and a physician assistant to perform minor surgical procedures;<hr><ltbullet>repeals the authority of an advanced practice registered nurse and a physician assistant to perform minor surgical procedures on July 1, 2028;<hr><ltbullet>clarifies collaboration requirements between an athletic trainer and a physician;<hr><ltbullet>allows an occupational therapist to prescribe durable medical equipment and adaptive devices;<hr><ltbullet>gives the division rulemaking authority for occupational therapists;<hr><ltbullet>directs the division to create and issue a respiratory care apprentice license;<hr><ltbullet>reduces the number of hours required for a physician assistant to work without a collaborative agreement;<hr><ltbullet>allows an acupuncturist to delegate a low-risk task to an unlicensed aide under specified conditions;<hr><ltbullet>changes the training requirements for an acupuncturist performing injection therapy;<hr><ltbullet>gives the division rulemaking authority to determine additional substances an acupuncturist may inject;<hr><ltbullet>clarifies the titles an acupuncturist may use;<hr><ltbullet>aligns dry needling for occupational therapists and physical therapists; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical and conforming changes.
